description: Wells Fargo employees opened millions of unauthorized accounts to meet sales targets. Public trust collapsed from ~35% favorable to ~15%. CEO John Stumpf resigned. $185M initial fine, eventually $3B+ in settlements. Tests corporate trust destruction cascade.

key_events.description: CFPB announces $185M fine. News breaks that 5,300 employees fired for opening 2+ million unauthorized accounts since 2011.
CEO John Stumpf testifies before Senate Banking Committee. Elizabeth Warren tells him he should resign and face criminal investigation. Viral moment.
Stumpf resigns as CEO and chairman. Clawback of $41M in compensation. Board investigation launched.
Scope widens: investigation reveals 3.5M fake accounts (up from 2M), fake insurance policies, and unauthorized mortgage modifications.
Fed imposes unprecedented asset cap on Wells Fargo (Feb 2018), capping growth at $1.95 trillion until compliance improvements.

calibration_notes: Tests corporate crisis with cascading revelations and institutional punishment. Unlike a single-event crisis, Wells Fargo suffered waves of expanding scope (2M to 3.5M accounts, then insurance fraud, then mortgage fraud). Key dynamics: each revelation preventing trust recovery, Congressional testimony as amplification event, regulatory escalation (fine to asset cap), leadership change failing to restore confidence, systemic vs individual framing of blame, and long-tail reputational damage persisting years after initial event.
